title: NEBExpress Ni Resin Batch Binding Protocol
author: New England Biolabs
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfd5ji86

steps.instruction: If continuing with Denaturing conditions: Bring all three buffers (Lysis/Binding, Wash and Elution Buffers) to a final concentration of 8 Molarity (M), Urea or 6 Molarity (M), Guanidine . Notes: 1.Crude lysate should be prepared with a lysis buffer without imidazole. To further minimize contaminants in the eluate, the concentration of imidazole in the wash buffer can be increased to 15 mM (≥15 mM can reduce the isolated yield but may result in increased purity). Imidazole concentration should be determined empirically. 2. Refer to the Chemical Compatibility table prior to including other additives.

Harvest cells by centrifugation at 4000 x g, °C, 15 minutes , store the pellet at -20 °C or process immediately. Note: it is recommended to pre-weigh the vessel prior to addition of cell suspension, in order to determine the mass of cell pellet used.

Resuspend cell pellet in Lysis buffer and lyse using method of choice (use approximately 5 mL, lysis buffer per 1 g, cell paste ). Note: Cells can be lysed by standard methods including sonication, repeated freeze-thaw cycles, French press, etc. Other commercially available lysis reagents can also be used, following manufacturer’s instructions. It is recommended that imidazole be omitted from any lysis buffer.

Centrifuge sample at 12000 x g, °C, 15 minutes to pellet cellular debris. Remove the clarified protein lysate supernatant and transfer to a new microcentrifuge tube on ice, retain an aliquot of the clarified lysate for SDS-PAGE analysis.
Gently shake the Ni Resin bottle to completely resuspend the slurry and transfer desired amount to a tube.
Settle the resin by centrifugation at 500 x g, °C, 5 minutes , remove the supernatant.
Equilibrate the resin by adding 5 column volumes (CV) of Lysis/Binding buffer, shake gently to mix.
Settle the resin by centrifugation at 500 x g, °C, 5 minutes , remove the supernatant.
Repeat equilibration steps once:

Add the protein sample lysate to the equilibrated resin. Mix gently by shaking at 200 rpm on a rotary shaker for ≥ 00:15:00 . Note: Binding of some His-tagged proteins can be increased with longer incubation times. Resin should be placed at 4 °C when extending the binding time to maintain activity of the target protein. Prolonged incubation may result in more non-specific binding.
Pour the lysate/resin mixture into an empty and closed gravity column.
Open the column and collect the flow through in a clean tube. Reserve for SDS-PAGE analysis.
Add ≥ 10 CV of Wash buffer to the column, allow buffer to flow through. Flow through can be monitored with absorbance at 280 nm to ensure complete removal of unbound lysate effluent.
Collect flow through in fractions and reserve for SDS-PAGE analysis.

Elute fusion protein with Elution buffer. Note: it is recommended to use a minimum of 3 CV of elution buffer to elute the target protein. Optimal amounts may need to be determined empirically for different substrates.
Collect flow through in fractions and monitor protein elution by measuring the absorbance at 280 nm or by SDS-PAGE.

Analyze the protein sample lysate (load), flow through, washes and eluates by SDS-PAGE. Note: To remove imidazole for downstream applications use gel filtration (e.g., Zeba Spin Desalting Columns) or dialysis (e.g. slide-A-Lyzer Dialysis Cassettes)

When using a gravity column, the flow rate should be determined prior to cleaning to ensure an accurate contact time with the cleaning agent. To remove lipoproteins, lipids and proteins strongly bound by hydrophobic interaction follow one of these three methods: Add 1 Molarity (M), NaOH to the column and allow a contact time of 01:00:00 to 02:00:00 , followed by 10 CV of Lysis/Binding Buffer. Add 30% isopropanol to the column and allow a contact time of 00:15:00 to 00:20:00 , followed by 10 CV of water. Add 0.1 – 0.5% non-ionic detergent in 0.1 Molarity (M), acetic acid to the column and allow a contact time of 01:00:00 to 02:00:00 , followed by 10 CV of water.
To remove proteins bound by ionic interactions Add 1.5 Molarity (M), NaCl to the column and allow a contact time of 00:10:00 to 00:15:00 . followed by 10 CV of water.
For long-term storage, NEBExpress Ni Resin should be stored in 20% ethanol at 2 °C to 8 °C ..
Settle the resin by centrifugation at 500 x g, °C, 5 minutes , remove the supernatant.
Equilibrate the resin by adding 5 column volumes (CV) of Lysis/Binding buffer, shake gently to mix.
Settle the resin by centrifugation at 500 x g, °C, 5 minutes , remove the supernatant.
